name: zitadel-idp
description: Zitadel self-hosted OIDC integration field guide — 28 documented quirks with proto-aligned examples verified against raw GitHub Zitadel v4.15.0. Captures the full set of high-friction issues discovered while integrating Zitadel v4.x as the IdP for JRC Brasil ERP — FirstInstance env placement, volume permissions, v1/v2 API split, tenant→orgId mapping, JWT validation traps over self-signed HTTPS (NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, JWKS over HTTPS), loginV2 instance flag, silent-renew redirect URI byte-match, JWT access token sem profile claims, idempotent bootstrap 'No changes' 400, TLS-terminating reverse proxy, secure-context PKCE on LAN, boot-time signinSilent recursion, StrictMode + closure cancelled flag, post_logout_redirect_uri invalid, branding da Login UI v1 / privateLabelingSetting / 405 em /assets/v1, custom_login_text e LANG-lg4DP, F5 com InMemoryWebStorage, 401 storm pós --reset-zitadel por tsx-watch zumbi, regressão multi-app YAML que sobrescreve env vars OIDC_REDIRECT_URIS dinâmicas, Zitadel v2.66.x panic 'No master key provided' (fix com flag CLI '--masterkey'); v0.3.0 — full v2.66 → v4 upgrade runbook + API v1 → v2 Connect protocol mapping; v0.4.0 — proto-aligned payloads verificados contra raw GitHub v4.15.0 (CreateApplication discriminator é oidcConfiguration top-level com applicationType / developmentMode internos, NÃO oidc com appType / devMode); per-service ListResponse field names (projects[] / projectRoles[] / applications[] / authorizations[] vs result[] genérico); CreateAuthorization exige organizationId, Update/Delete usam id (não authorizationId), shape Authorization é nested (project.id, user.id, roles[].key); AlreadyExisting (com ing) no matcher de idempotência; quirk 28 — Login UI v2 auto-provisioning quebrado em v4.15.0 (zitadel/zitadel#8910 / #9293 — adicionar LOGINCLIENT_MACHINE_* envs causa unique_constraints_pkey duplicate em 03_default_instance migration, sem essas envs o zitadel-login fica em loop 'Awaiting file and reading token' eternamente — mitigação canônica é Path B do quirk 25 com loginV2.required: false). v0.6.0 — Console UI human-user creation pitfalls (3 new quirks, all triggered by manual operator workflows, none affecting API/bootstrap-driven creation): quirk 33 — Console v4 'Add Human User' form auto-truncates Username to email's local-part on auto-fill, combined with userLoginMustBeDomain=false default this makes the canonical loginName drop the @domain, end users get 'O usuário não pôde ser encontrado' / 'User could not be found' when logging in with their full email; quirk 34 — userLoginMustBeDomain=true (Domain settings 'Add Organization Domain as suffix to loginnames') stamps loginNames irreversibly, disabling+Reset to Instance default does NOT undo, doubly hostile in self-hosted because org primaryDomain auto-generates as <slug>.<externalDomain> not the company email domain (so suffix is nonsensical or double-suffixed); quirk 35 — Console 'E-mail Verificado' checkbox is OFF by default, first login then triggers SMTP verification code that never arrives if SMTP isn't configured (common in self-hosted early-stage), user stuck on 'code sent' screen forever. Bundles a working docker-compose.zitadel.yml, idempotent bootstrap-zitadel.ts (annotated with v2 equivalents) and reset-zitadel.sh script. Use whenever starting Zitadel integration, debugging 4xx on Zitadel APIs, validating Zitadel JWTs in Node/TypeScript, customizing the hosted Login UI, wiring an SPA via OIDC PKCE, planning a v2.66→v4 upgrade, refactoring callers from API v1 to v2 against v4.15+, hitting the Login UI v2 deploy bug, or onboarding TI staff to provision human users via Console (Console form has 3 silent footguns).
